content.problemStatement:
Chef has moved to a new office where the monthly cost is **1000 rupees per employee seat**.
If Chef’s company has xxx **employees**, determine the **total monthly rent** Chef has to pay.

Function DeclarationFunction NamecalculateMonthlyPaymentcalculateMonthlyPaymentcalculateMonthlyPayment – This function calculates the total monthly office payment based on the number of employee seats.

Parameters
xxx : The number of employees in the company.
Return Value
Returns an integer representing the total monthly payment in rupees.
Constraints
1≤x≤1001 \leq x \leq 1001≤x≤100

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer xxx — the number of employees.


Output Format

Print a single integer representing the monthly payment Chef has to make.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer x — the number of employees for that test case.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output a single line containing the total monthly rent in rupees, which equals x × 1000.

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is a single test case with 1 employee. Total rent = 1 × 1000 = 1000 rupees.
One test case, 25 employees. Total rent = 25 × 1000 = 25000 rupees.
One test case, 100 employees. Total rent = 100 × 1000 = 100000 rupees.

solution.approach: For each test case we read the integer x and multiply it by the fixed cost per seat (1000). The product is printed on its own line. This is O(1) work per test case, so overall O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
